qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation during SSA codegen for repeated array literals
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns SSA codegen materializing repeated array literals via repeat_n(...).collect() in compiler/noirc_evaluator/src/ssa/ssa_gen/mod.rs, whereas the advisory concerns allocate_foreign_call_result_array() under-allocating nested foreign-call result arrays by using semantic nested_size instead of semi-flattened composite slot count. The findings are in the same broader Noir ecosystem but identify distinct code and root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Brillig heap corruption root cause. The advisory is specifically about nested array allocation for foreign-call results in Brillig using semantic nested array length instead of the semi-flattened slot count for tuple/composite elements. The qualifying finding instead describes a distinct Noir SSA repeated-array literal memory exhaustion issue.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-jj7c-x25r-r8r3 describes allocate_external_call_results() and allocate_foreign_call_result_array() in Brillig foreign-call result code, with nested Type::Array(_, nested_size) allocation under-counting tuple/composite element slots.

Finding unbounded_array_allocation_dos reports compiler/noirc_evaluator/src/ssa/ssa_gen/mod.rs repeat_n(element_value, *length as usize).collect() for repeated array literals, not Brillig foreign-call result heap allocation.
